LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. AT THE REQUEST OF COUNSEL, THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS UNITED SERVICES AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS CONTINUED TO MAY 9, 2014 TO GIVE ALL PARTIES THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1110(F) AND LOCAL RULE 2.6B. THE PARTIES MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF ALL PAPERS FILED IN SUPPORT OF AND OPPOSITION TO UNITED SERVICES AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION, TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN MAY 1, 2014 AT 4:00 PM. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976.

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL REPRESENTED TO THE COURT THAT DEFENDANT AND MOVING PARTY USAA CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY HAS BEEN DISMISSED FROM THE CASE. THEREFORE, THE COURT VACATED THE TENTATIVE RULING AND ORDERED USAA CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OFF CALENDAR. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT UNITED SERVICES AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. THE UNDISPUTED EVIDENCE DEMONSTRATES THAT PLAINTIFF REQUESTED THAT DEFENDANT EXHAUST THE INSURANCE POLICY LIMITS TO SETTLE THE LILJESTRAND LITIGATION AND DEFENDANT COMPLIED WITH PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST. DEFENDANT DISCHARGED ITS OBLIGATIONS UNDER THE POLICIES. WHEN THE LILJESTRAND LITIGATION WAS SETTLED, MS. CURRAN STATED THAT SHE DID NOT PLAN TO FILE A CLAIM. DEFENDANT DID NOT ACT IN BAD FAITH BY SETTLING THE ONLY KNOWN CLAIM AGAINST ITS INSURED. IN ADDITION, PLAINTIFF HAS WAIVED ITS RIGHT TO SUE DEFENDANT OVER ITS HANDLING OF THE LILJESTRAND CASE BECAUSE DEFENDANT COMPLIED WITH PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST TO EXHAUST THE POLICIES. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: JOYCE FINLEY #8620. (302)
MINI MINUTES FOR MAY-20-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summ Adjudication
Off Calendar May-20-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: ROBERT CHIN, ESQ., 415.256.8188, FOR PLAINTIFF MERVYN SILBERBERG.
ROBERT MCLAY, ESQ., 650.637.9100, FOR DEFENDANT UNITED SERVICES AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT UNITED SERVICES AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. The undisputed evidence demonstrates that plaintiff requested that defendant exhaust the insurance policy limits to settle the Liljestrand litigation and defendant complied with plaintiff's request. Defendant discharged its obligations under the policies. When the Liljestrand litigation was settled, Ms. Curran stated that she did not plan to file a claim. Defendant did not act in bad faith by settling the only known claim against its insured. In addition, plaintiff has waived its right to sue defendant over its handling of the Liljestrand case because defendant complied with plaintiff's request to exhaust the policies. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Joyce Finley #8620.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions, Or In The Alternative, Dismissal Of Plaintiffs Financial Loss Claims
Off Calendar Mar-07-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: ROBERT V. CHIN, ESQ., 415.256.8188, FOR PLAINTIFF MERVYN SILBERBERG.
ROBERT S. MCLAY, ESQ., 650.637.9100, FOR DEFENDANTS UNITED SERVICES AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION AND USAA CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY.

RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS UNITED SERVICES AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION AND USAA CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY'S And Motion For Terminating Sanctions, Or In The Alternative, Dismissal Of Plaintiff's Financial Loss Claims is granted in part. The Court finds that plaintiff, in making various claims for financial loss and loss of business opportunities, has placed his financial condition in issue and therefore waived, at least in part, his privacy rights as to his relevant financial documents. Plaintiff alleges he suffered financial losses due to lack of funds, which he alleges was caused by his settlement of the Curran claim. Plaintiff has a relatively complicated array of financial holdings. For USAA to defend itself, it would need all documents necessary for its experts to conduct a complete analysis of plaintiff's financial condition. Defendant's Demands for Production (RPD) Nos. 24 and 25 are aimed at discovering those financial documents supporting plaintiff's case, which would include checking/savings/financial statements, profit and loss statements for the businesses for which he claims he sustained losses, document showing cash flow (income and expenses) for the various rental properties, and proof of lost future business opportunities, such as correspondence, finance/credit applications, proposed contracts, etc. This Court's prior order of 11/4/13 afforded plaintiff one last opportunity to fully respond to RPD 24 and 25. The evidence shows he has not complied with the prior order. Under these circumstances, an evidentiary sanction is appropriate under CCP 2023.030(c). Therefore, the Court imposes evidence sanctions as follows: The Court orders that plaintiff shall be precluded at trial from claiming any damages for economic loss other than the $500,000 settlement of the Curran case, and attorney's fees and costs. This order does not affect claims for non-economic damages. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM CHARLES GEERHART, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.
